sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see something important! Looking at the image, the blocks are now at the TOP of the left chamber, near the white cross. The orange part is at the top, and the blue part is below it.
But more importantly, looking at the ICON at the bottom-left, the blue shape has changed! It now shows a different pattern. The blue shape now has a horizontal part at the top and a vertical part on the left.
This is significant! The icon changes when the blocks move to different positions. This suggests that the icon is a "progress tracker" or "goal tracker" that shows the block position relative to the maze.
Looking at the icon, the blue shape now shows:
- A horizontal part at the top (rows 55-56, cols 3-7)
- A vertical part on the left (rows 55-60, cols 3-4)
This is an "L" shape, but rotated compared to the previous icon.
Wait, actually the icon still shows an "L" shape, but the blue part has changed. Let me look more carefully.
Actually, looking at the icon, the blue shape now shows:
- A horizontal part at the top (rows 55-56, cols 3-7)
- A vertical part on the left (rows 55-60, cols 3-4)
This is an "L" shape, with the horizontal part at the top and the vertical part on the left.
Comparing to the previous icon, the blue shape has changed. This suggests that the icon is tracking the block position.
Now, the blocks are at the top of the left chamber, near the white cross. The icon has changed to show a different pattern. This might be the goal state!
Let me check if the level is complete. The result shows `level_completed: false`, so the level is not complete yet.
But the icon has changed, which is progress. Let me continue moving the blocks to see if the icon changes further.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the icon more carefully. The icon shows a blue "L" shape, but the blue part has changed. The blue part now shows a horizontal line at the top and a vertical line on the left.
This might represent the block position in the maze. The blocks are at the top of the left chamber, and the icon shows a horizontal line at the top and a vertical line on the left.
If the icon is a mini-map, then the blue shape represents the block position, and the B/b colors represent the maze layout.
But I don't know how the icon relates to the block positions. Let me try to infer it from the game state.
Actually, I think the key insight is that the icon changes when the blocks move to different positions. The icon might be a "goal tracker" that shows how close the blocks are to the goal position.
If the icon is a goal tracker, then the goal might be to move the blocks to a position where the icon shows a specific pattern.
Let me try moving the blocks to different positions and see how the icon changes.
Actually, I just noticed something. The blocks are now at the top of the left chamber, near the white cross. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 19-23 (approximately).
The blocks are close to the white cross, but not exactly at the same position. Let me try moving the blocks to the white cross position.
The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 19-23. To move the blocks to the white cross, I need to move DOWN and RIGHT.
Let me try moving DOWN and RIGHT.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Blocks are at rows 25-29, cols 19-23 (top of left chamber)
# White cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22
# Try moving DOWN to align with the white cross
action(['DOWN'] * 3)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
